    - The buffer_adaptor provides size() and free() methods which return the current size and free space respectively.
    - We'll create a BufferInfo struct and fill it with these values.

 - For writing the byte array, we use `buffer.write(data, length)`.

 - For reading the integer, we use `buffer.read(&value, sizeof(value))`.
 - For reading the byte array, we use `buffer.read(data, length)`.

 - The get_buffer_info function simply returns {buffer.size(), buffer.free()}.

#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <array>
#include <cstdint>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<std::size_t Size>
constexpr auto create_filled_array(std::uint8_t value) {
    std::array<std::uint8_t, Size> target{};
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < Size; ++i) {
        target[i] = value;
    }
    return target;
}

bool verify_filled_array(const auto& arr, std::uint8_t expected_value) {
    for (const auto& elem : arr) {
        if (elem != expected_value) {
            return false;
        }
    }
    return true;
}
```

Alternatively, for the verification function, we can use the `std::ranges::all_of` algorithm:

```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <array>
#include <cstdint>
#include <ranges>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<std::size_t Size>
constexpr auto create_filled_array(std::uint8_t value) {
    std::array<std::uint8_t, Size> target{};
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < Size; ++i) {
        target[i] = value;
    }
    return target;
}

bool verify_filled_array(const auto& arr, std::uint8_t expected_value) {
    return std::ranges::all_of(arr, [&expected_value](std::uint8_t elem) {
        return elem == expected_value;
    });
}
```

We are given the task to implement three functions for serializing, deserializing, and extracting the header of a ProtocolPacket using the Hexi library, with explicit endianness conversion to big-endian for network transmission.

### Approach

1. **Serialization (`serialize_protocol_packet`)**:
   - Convert `message_id` from native to big-endian using `hexi::endian::native_to_big`.
   - Write the converted `message_id` to the stream.
   - Convert each element in `data_values` from native to big-endian and write them using `write_container`.
   - Convert `checksum` from native to big-endian and write it.

2. **Deserialization (`deserialize_protocol_packet`)**:
   - Read the `message_id` and convert it from big-endian to native using `hexi::endian::big_to_native`.
   - Read the count of `data_values` (which is a uint32_t) and convert it to native.
   - Use `read_container` to read the `data_values` vector, then convert each element from big-endian to native.
   - Read the `checksum` and convert it from big-endian to native.

3. **Header Extraction (`extract_protocol_header`)**:
   - Use `span` to get a view of the first 4 bytes (message_id) and convert it from big-endian to native.
   - Skip the first 4 bytes to avoid reading them again.

### Solution Code

```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct ProtocolPacket {
    uint32_t message_id;
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    uint32_t checksum;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_protocol_packet(const ProtocolPacket& packet) {
    hexi::dynamic_buffer<128> buffer; // Using dynamic_buffer as the underlying storage
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);

    // Serialize message_id with big-endian conversion
    uint32_t big_endian_message_id = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.message_id);
    stream.write(&big_endian_message_id, sizeof(big_endian_message_id));

    // Serialize data_values with big-endian conversion
    std::vector<uint16_t> big_endian_data_values;
    big_endian_data_values.reserve(packet.data_values.size());
    for (uint16_t value : packet.data_values) {
        big_endian_data_values.push_back(hexi::endian::native_to_big(value));
    }
    stream.write_container(big_endian_data_values);

    // Serialize checksum with big-endian conversion
    uint32_t big_endian_checksum = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.checksum);
    stream.write(&big_endian_checksum, sizeof(big_endian_checksum));

    return buffer.to_vector();
}

ProtocolPacket deserialize_protocol_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::static_buffer<std::uint8_t, 128> static_buf;
    static_buf.write(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    hexi::binary_stream stream(static_buf);

    ProtocolPacket packet;

    // Deserialize message_id
    uint32_t message_id;
    stream.read(&message_id, sizeof(message_id));
    packet.message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(message_id);

    // Deserialize data_values count
    uint32_t count;
    stream.read(&count, sizeof(count));
    count = hexi::endian::big_to_native(count);

    // Deserialize data_values
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values(count);
    stream.read_container(data_values);
    for (uint16_t& value : data_values) {
        value = hexi::endian::big_to_native(value);
    }
    packet.data_values = std::move(data_values);

    // Deserialize checksum
    uint32_t checksum;
    stream.read(&checksum, sizeof(checksum));
    packet.checksum = hexi::endian::big_to_native(checksum);

    return packet;
}

struct ProtocolHeader {
    uint32_t message_id;
};

ProtocolHeader extract_protocol_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::static_buffer<std::uint8_t, 4> static_buf;
    static_buf.write(buffer.data(), 4);
    hexi::binary_stream stream(static_buf);

    ProtocolHeader header;
    uint32_t message_id;
    stream.read(&message_id, sizeof(message_id));
    header.message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(message_id);

    // Skip the remaining bytes if needed, but since we only read 4, we can just return
    return header;
}
```

### Explanation

- **Serialization**: We create a `hexi::dynamic_buffer` to hold the serialized data. We then use `hexi::binary_stream` to write each field after converting it to big-endian. The `write_container` method is used for the vector to handle the entire container efficiently.

- **Deserialization**: We use a `hexi::static_buffer` to hold the input buffer and then read each field, converting from big-endian to native. The count of `data_values` is read first to know how many elements to expect, then the vector is read and each element is converted.

- **Header Extraction**: We use a small `hexi::static_buffer` to read only the first 4 bytes (the message_id), convert it, and then return the header. The `skip` method is not explicitly needed here because we only read the required bytes.

This approach ensures that all endianness conversions are done explicitly using the provided functions from the Hexi library, adhering to the network protocol requirements.

   The code:

```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <string>
#include <cstring>

struct MeasurementData {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::vector<float> temperatures;
    double pressure;

    void serialise(auto& stream) const {
        // Write sensor_id directly
        stream.write(sensor_id);

        // Write each temperature after converting from native to little-endian
        for (float temp : temperatures) {
            float converted = hexi::endian::conditional_reverse<std::endian::native, std::endian::little>(temp);
            stream.write(converted);
        }

        // Convert pressure and write
        double converted_pressure = hexi::endian::conditional_reverse<std::endian::native, std::endian::little>(pressure);
        stream.write(converted_pressure);
    }

    void deserialise(auto& stream) {
        // Read sensor_id directly
        stream.read(sensor_id);

        // Read each temperature and convert from little-endian to native in-place
        for (float& temp : temperatures) {
            stream.read(temp);
            hexi::endian::little_to_native_inplace(temp);
        }

        // Read pressure and convert in-place
        stream.read(pressure);
        hexi::endian::little_to_native_inplace(pressure);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_measurement_data(const MeasurementData& data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);

    stream.serialise(data);
    return buffer;
}

MeasurementData deserialize_measurement_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);

    MeasurementData data;
    stream.deserialise(data);
    return data;
}

struct SensorInfo {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::string_view sensor_name;
};

SensorInfo extract_sensor_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);

    SensorInfo info;

    // Extract sensor name
    const char* data = static_cast<const char*>(stream.view());
    const char* null_pos = std::strchr(data, '\0');
    if (null_pos != nullptr) {
        info.sensor_name = std::string(data, null_pos - data);
        stream.skip(null_pos - data + 1); // skip the null terminator
    } else {
        info.sensor_name = "";
    }

    // Check and read sensor_id
    stream.enforce_read_bounds(sizeof(uint32_t));
    stream.read(&info.sensor_id, sizeof(uint32_t));

    // Skip the remaining data
    stream.skip(buffer.size() - stream.total_read());

    return info;
}

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <string>
#include <cstring>

struct MeasurementData {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::vector<float> temperatures;
    double pressure;

    void serialise(auto& stream) const {
        // Write sensor_id directly
        stream.write(sensor_id);

        // Write each temperature after converting from native to little-endian
        for (float temp : temperatures) {
            float converted = hexi::endian::conditional_reverse<std::endian::native, std::endian::little>(temp);
            stream.write(converted);
        }

        // Convert pressure and write
        double converted_pressure = hexi::endian::conditional_reverse<std::endian::native, std::endian::little>(pressure);
        stream.write(converted_pressure);
    }

    void deserialise(auto& stream) {
        // Read sensor_id directly
        stream.read(sensor_id);

        // Read each temperature and convert from little-endian to native in-place
        for (float& temp : temperatures) {
            stream.read(temp);
            hexi::endian::little_to_native_inplace(temp);
        }

        // Read pressure and convert in-place
        stream.read(pressure);
        hexi::endian::little_to_native_inplace(pressure);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_measurement_data(const MeasurementData& data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);

    stream.serialise(data);
    return buffer;
}

MeasurementData deserialize_measurement_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);

    MeasurementData data;
    stream.deserialise(data);
    return data;
}

struct SensorInfo {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::string_view sensor_name;
};

SensorInfo extract_sensor_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);

    SensorInfo info;

    // Extract sensor name
    const char* data = static_cast<const char*>(stream.view());
    const char* null_pos = std::strchr(data, '\0');
    if (null_pos != nullptr) {
        info.sensor_name = std::string(data, null_pos - data);
        stream.skip(null_pos - data + 1); // skip the null terminator
    } else {
        info.sensor_name = "";
    }

    // Check and read sensor_id
    stream.enforce_read_bounds(sizeof(uint32_t));
    stream.read(&info.sensor_id, sizeof(uint32_t));

    // Skip the remaining data
    stream.skip(buffer.size() - stream.total_read());

    return info;
}
```</answer>
